mechanism_narrative: Sustained high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes consumer purchasing power and increases corporate input costs, leading to margin compression and ultimately negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above its 10-year average, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, which typically precedes a decline in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points from its 3-month low
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return following triggers is not statistically significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an actively rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an overheating economy, prompting the central bank to implement further aggressive monetary tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS changes is not statistically significant in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production (INDPRO) during an economic expansion signifies strong underlying corporate health and demand, leading to improved earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: During periods officially designated as economic expansion, INDPRO growth does not correlate positively with SPX 60d returns, or average returns are flat.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) provides an accommodative financial environment, reducing corporate borrowing costs and encouraging investment, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, or the average 5d return is not statistically significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which translates into stronger consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS growth is strong, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX returns is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals a contraction in construction activity and broader economic demand, leading to concerns about future growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of periods following a significant HOUST decline, or the average return is not statistically significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: High industrial production (INDPRO) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) suggests supply-side constraints are driving prices up, forcing the central bank to tighten monetary policy more aggressively, negatively impacting equities over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 105 AND CPIAUCSL > 290 AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the combined effect of these proxies does not significantly predict negative returns.
